metric_value: Pocket PC will eclipse Palm OS as the leading handheld platform by 2005 — the study's title-level claim
notes: Primary falsified prediction; used as prescience case study

metric_value: Palm OS showing varying levels of enterprise success contrary to its market share position
notes: Enterprise adoption lagging consumer market share for Palm OS

metric_value: Higher enterprise adoption rate than Palm OS despite smaller total market share
notes: Core evidence for the Pocket PC enterprise dominance prediction

metric_value: Enterprises select PDA platforms based on operating system; Pocket PC benefits from Microsoft relationship
notes: IT alignment with Windows infrastructure cited as enterprise advantage

metric_name: Palm OS consumer vs enterprise split
metric_value: Palm OS success in consumer does not translate directly to enterprise adoption
notes: Brand awareness-market share disconnect theme consistent with companion b2c1d0 study

metric_name: Compaq iPAQ as leading Pocket PC hardware
metric_value: Compaq iPAQ identified as leading Pocket PC hardware driving enterprise Pocket PC adoption
notes: iPAQ was dominant Pocket PC device in 2001

metric_value: Enterprises beginning to purchase and deploy PDAs; supplier selection driven by OS
notes: 2001 was early phase of enterprise PDA adoption

metric_name: Palm OS total market share vs enterprise share
metric_value: Consumer market share dominance does not reflect enterprise adoption; run contrary to respective market shares
notes: Discrepancy between consumer and enterprise share was accurate observation

metric_value: Pocket PC name dropped 2007; superseded by Windows Mobile then Windows Phone 2010; never became the dominant enterprise handheld
notes: iPhone June 2007 rendered the enterprise PDA competition moot before either platform could win

metric_value: Apple launched iPhone June 29 2007 creating entirely new smartphone/handheld paradigm; both Pocket PC and Palm OS made irrelevant
notes: iPhone sold 1.39M units in Q3 2007; by 2010 both Pocket PC and Palm OS effectively dead

metric_value: Palm acquired by HP in 2010 for $1.2 billion; webOS failed; Palm brand retired 2011
notes: Palm's smartphone pivot (Treo) briefly successful but unable to compete with iPhone/Android

metric_value: Windows Mobile market share collapsed from 23% in 2004 to 7.9% in Q3 2009; Windows Phone announced February 2010
notes: iPhone and Android jointly destroyed Windows Mobile enterprise position despite Aberdeen's 2001 prediction
